sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è decisamente risk-off oggi, con il VIX che sale dell'11.31% a 26.78, livello che indica un mercato difensivo. Il DXY scende leggermente, ma rimane sopra 99, mentre il prezzo del petrolio (Brent) scende del 5.15% a $106.41. Gli indici azionari (S&P 500, STOXX 600, NIKKEI) sono in calo, con perdite comprese tra l'1.51% e il 3.38%.
sentiment.reason_en: The market sentiment is strongly risk-off today, with the VIX rising 11.31% to 26.78, indicating a defensive market. The DXY slightly decreases but remains above 99, while the oil price (Brent) drops 5.15% to $106.41. Equity indices (S&P 500, STOXX 600, NIKKEI) are down, with losses ranging from 1.51% to 3.38%.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario è in calo, con S&P 500 -1.51% e STOXX 600 -1.77%. Il VIX sale dell'11.31% a 26.78. Il prezzo del petrolio (Brent) scende del 5.15% a $106.41. L'oro guadagna lo 0.10% a $4574.90/oz. Le criptovalute mostrano segnali contrastanti, con Bitcoin sostanzialmente stabile intorno ai $70,000.
market_impact_summary.en: The stock market is down, with S&P 500 -1.51% and STOXX 600 -1.77%. The VIX rises 11.31% to 26.78. The oil price (Brent) drops 5.15% to $106.41. Gold gains 0.10% to $4574.90/oz. Cryptocurrencies show mixed signals, with Bitcoin essentially stable around $70,000.

audio_script_it:
Mentre le borse mondiali osservano la consueta pausa festiva del fine settimana, i riflettori restano accesi sul comparto digitale che opera 24 ore su 24. La seduta asiatica, che ha chiuso prima della pausa, ci consegna un Nikkei in calo del 3.38% a 53,373 punti e Shanghai sostanzialmente invariato a 3957.05 punti. Questi trend asiatici anticipano una possibile apertura cauta delle borse europee e americane quando riapriranno.

Il sentiment è decisamente risk-off, come evidenziato dai dati di mercato: l'S&P 500 futures indica una possibile apertura in negativo, il Dollar Index (DXY) sale a 99.50, mentre il VIX, indice della paura, schizza al 26.78, +11.31%. I tassi di interesse continuano a salire, con il rendimento del decennale USA al 4.39%.

Geopoliticamente, l'attenzione è alta sul conflitto in corso, con notizie di un possibile aumento delle tensioni che stanno facendo aumentare il prezzo del petrolio e dell'oro.

Sul fronte macroeconomico, l'ultimo dato sul PIL dell'Eurozona ha mostrato una crescita dello 0.2%, mentre il tasso BCE è salito al 2.00%. Negli Usa, i dati più recenti mostrano un tasso di disoccupazione al 4.4% e un Core CPI YoY al 2.7%.

Passando al comparto digitale, Bitcoin quota $70,382 con una variazione negativa del 0.20%, mentre Ethereum e altre criptovalute mostrano trend positivi. L'indice Fear & Greed è a 12, in territorio di Extreme Fear.

In questo contesto, l'attenzione degli investitori rimane focalizzata sull'evoluzione del conflitto e sui prossimi dati macroeconomici che saranno rilasciati la prossima settimana. La liquidità globale, misurata dal Global M2, continua ad espandersi, +4.3% YoY. 

Per la giornata odierna, quindi, ci si concentra principalmente sull'andamento delle criptovalute e sugli sviluppi geopolitici che potrebbero influenzare i mercati nella prossima settimana.

{'intro': "Passiamo ora al comparto degli asset digitali, con un'analisi approfondita delle criptovalute.", 'deep_dive_bitcoin': "Iniziamo con il Bitcoin, che attualmente si attesta a $70,383, con un incremento dell'1.21%. Gli ETF Bitcoin hanno registrato un flusso netto di $46 milioni ieri, secondo i dati di mercato. Questo indica un interesse ancora presente nel mercato, nonostante le recenti fluttuazioni.", 'altcoins': "Passando alle altcoin, Ethereum guadagna l'1.04% arrivando a $2,148, mentre Solana cresce dell'1.01% a $89.60. Binance Coin (BNB) registra un aumento dello 0.49% a $641.49. Questi movimenti sono in linea con la tendenza generale del mercato crypto, che sembra essersi stabilizzato dopo le recenti turbolenze.", 'sentiment_fear_greed': "L'indice Fear & Greed è attualmente a 12, in territorio di Extreme Fear. Questo livello di paura estrema potrebbe indicare una possibile inversione di tendenza nel breve termine, poiché gli investitori tendono ad essere eccessivamente pessimisti in questi momenti. La correlazione con i dati macroeconomici, come l'inflazione e i tassi di interesse, rimane alta, e gli investitori continuano a monitorare questi indicatori per prendere decisioni informate.", 'conclusione': "In sintesi, il mercato delle criptovalute sembra essersi stabilizzato, con il Bitcoin e le altcoin che registrano piccoli guadagni. Tuttavia, l'estremo livello di paura tra gli investitori e la continua incertezza macroeconomica potrebbero influenzare le dinamiche del mercato nei prossimi giorni."}

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
{'introduction': "Welcome to your daily morning market briefing. Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.", 'sentiment': 'Global sentiment is risk-off today, with investors digesting recent economic data and geopolitical tensions. Key data points include the US Core CPI YoY at 2.7% and the recent PCE Core YoY at 3.1%.', 'traditional_markets': 'Traditional markets are closed for the weekend, but looking at the last available data: Equities were down, with the S&P 500 at 6506.48 (-1.51%) and the STOXX 600 at 56.70 (-1.77%). Bonds were also under pressure, with the US 10Y Yield at 4.39% (+2.57%) and the TLT Bond USA 20Y at 85.83 (-1.90%). Currencies saw the EUR/USD at 1.15 (-0.02%) and Commodities like GOLD at $4574.90/oz (+0.10%) and BRENT at $106.41 (-5.15%).', 'geopolitics_macro': "Geopolitical tensions, particularly with the ongoing situation in Iran, are influencing market sentiment and could have implications for inflation and interest rates. The recent decision by the ECB and the Bank of England's stance on rates are also being closely watched.", 'transition_crypto': "While traditional markets observe their holiday break, the spotlight remains on digital assets, which trade 24/7. Let's take a look at the current state of the crypto market.", 'crypto_market': 'The Fear & Greed Index is at 12, indicating Extreme Fear. Bitcoin is currently at $70,383 (+1.21%), with Ethereum at $2,148 (+1.04%), Solana at $89.60 (+1.01%), and Binance Coin at $641.49 (+0.49%). The BTC ETF Daily Net Inflow was $-52.0M, showing some improvement.'}

Moving on to the digital assets space, let's take a closer look at the current state of the cryptocurrency market. 

The Bitcoin price has been holding steady above $70,000, with a 1.21% increase to $70,383. From a technical analysis perspective, we can see that the recent price action has been characterized by a surge in buying activity, with a significant increase in inflows. However, the Fear & Greed Index is currently at 12, indicating extreme fear in the market.

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and examine some of the top altcoins. Ethereum, Solana, and Binance Coin have all seen moderate gains, with ETH up 1.04% to $2,148, SOL up 1.01% to $89.60, and BNB up 0.49% to $641.49. Ethereum's recent price action has been driven by increased adoption and usage of its network, while Solana's gains can be attributed to its growing popularity in the DeFi space.

In terms of market sentiment, the Fear & Greed Index is signaling extreme fear, which could be a contrarian indicator for a potential price rebound. The correlation between cryptocurrencies and traditional assets has been increasing, with many investors viewing Bitcoin as a risk-off asset. As we continue to monitor the market, it's essential to keep a close eye on macroeconomic indicators and their potential impact on the cryptocurrency space.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A closing note from Prais Alért, see you tomorrow.

articles.title: Conflict and Consequences
articles.snippet: Welcome to The Adversarial. Every other week, we’ll provide you with expert analysis on America’s greatest challengers: China, Russia, Iran, North Korea, and jihadists. Read more below.***IranOn March 17, Israel announced — and Tehran later confirmed — the killing of Ali Larijani, the secretary of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council. If eliminating Ali Khamenei on the opening day of the U.S.-Israeli offensive struck down the regime’s supreme leader, Larijani’s death removes its supreme coordinator. A longtime fixture in the regime, Larijani appears to have been serving as the regime’s majordomo in recent months — and especially since Khamenei’s death on Feb. The post Conflict and Consequences appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: What the Donroe Doctrine Could Mean for China’s Economic Statecraft
articles.snippet: In the first few months of 2026, the Trump administration has embarked on military interventions in Iran and Venezuela and threatened possible annexation of Greenland. Meanwhile, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s February trip to Beijing followed on the heels of the first state visit to China by a British leader in eight years. As the United States and China prepare for their much-touted summit in late March, Beijing is weighing its options in the face of recent American actions and the new “Donroe Doctrine.”Chinese foreign policy remains driven by the core priorities of preserving regime security and consolidating influence in its The post What the Donroe Doctrine Could Mean for China’s Economic Statecraft appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: Amid War, Yemen’s New Government Faces Challenges at Home and Abroad
articles.snippet: Yemen has a new government, formed after elements of the old one clashed on the battlefield. In this Q&A, Crisis Group expert Ahmed Nagi looks at its makeup and its prospects against the backdrop of Middle East war that may draw in the Houthi rebels.

articles.title: Why Did the United States Lift Sanctions on Assad’s Chemical Weapons Scientists?
articles.snippet: On June 30, 2025, as part of the process of normalizing relations with Damascus following the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad, the Trump administration overhauled the sanctions imposed on Syria to facilitate the rebuilding of that war-ravaged country. In the course of revising these sanctions, however, 266 employees of the Scientific Studies and Research Center, the heart of the Assad regime’s chemical weapons complex, were removed without explanation from the U.S. Department of the Treasury’s Specially Designated Nationals List. This list is a compilation of human rights abusers, proliferators, terrorists, drug lords, and others deemed a threat to U.S. The post Why Did the United States Lift Sanctions on Assad’s Chemical Weapons Scientists? appeared first on War on the Rocks

articles.title: Where is the Israel-Hizbollah War Going?

articles.title: Why the Iran War May Have Just Killed the AI Boom
articles.snippet: The stock market spent the first week of the Iran war doing something strange: mostly shrugging. Oil spiked. Insurance markets effectively collapsed. Amazon had two data centers blown up. And the Nasdaq dipped, steadied, and the conversation shifted within days to whether the Fed might still cut in June. The prevailing read was: disruption, yes. Catastrophe, no. This thing will be over soon. I think that read is wrong. And wrong in a specific, structural way, not because the war will necessarily escalate further, but because the damage…
articles.title_it: La guerra tra Israele e Iran potrebbe aver ucciso il boom dell'AI
articles.title_en: The Iran War May Have Just Killed the AI Boom

articles.summary_en: The increase in oil prices due to the Israel-Iran war could slow down the artificial intelligence boom.

articles.title: U.S. Bets on Natural Gas to Power 10 GW AI Buildout in Ohio
articles.snippet: Amid the Iranian war and oil price crisis, the Department of Energy, alongside the Department of Commerce, has unveiled a public-private partnership with SoftBank and AEP Ohio that centers on one thing the AI boom desperately needs but rarely talks about plainly—power. Not theoretical power. Not intermittent power. Real, dispatchable, grid-ready megawatts, mostly from natural gas. At the core of the plan, revealed in a Friday press release, is a 10-gigawatt data center campus at the Portsmouth Site in southern Ohio, backed by an equally massive…

articles.title: Why This Energy Shock Will Hit Consumers Harder Than 2011
articles.snippet: Arend Kapteyn, the global head of economics and strategy research and chief economist at UBS, told clients that one key reason the current Middle East conflict-driven energy shock "is not like 2011-2014" will be the absence of a comparable response from the shale patch, suggesting consumers are more likely to bear the brunt of the pain. Kapteyn noted that, on an inflation-adjusted basis, oil prices in 2011-2014 were actually higher than they are today, yet the U.S. economy absorbed that shock because the shale boom provided a lift to the…

articles.title: Oil Extends Rally as Hormuz Stays Closed
articles.snippet: Oil is heading for a fifth straight weekly gain, with Brent near $109, as the Strait of Hormuz remains shut for a third week, keeping supply tight despite policy efforts to ease the market. Friday, March 20, 2026 Oil markets are set to post their fifth consecutive weekly gain (excepting WTI which will see a minor week-over-week decline), with ICE Brent trading around $109 per barrel. Whilst the Trump administration has sought to defuse market concerns using any means available – waiving the Jones Act, easing Russian sanctions further or de-sanctioning…

articles.title: Bitcoin Price Holds $70,000 as War-Driven Inflation Fears Meet Defensive Market Positioning
articles.snippet: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Price Holds $70,000 as War-Driven Inflation Fears Meet Defensive Market Positioning Bitcoin price held near $70,000 as shifting Iran conflict risks and macro pressure weighed on markets, while derivatives and on-chain data signaled consolidation, not capitulation. This post Bitcoin Price Holds $70,000 as War-Driven Inflation Fears Meet Defensive Market Positioning first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

articles.title: Oil Prices Ease As US Pulls Out All Stops To Secure Supply
articles.snippet: Oil prices pulled back Friday, but not because the market suddenly feels safe. This is more of a tactical breather than a trend change. Brent slipped back toward the $109 level, while WTI hovered in the high-$90s, easing from the week’s Thursday spike that briefly pushed Brent near $120. The trigger wasn’t a shift in fundamentals—it was policy. Washington is scrambling to throw barrels at the problem, and allies are lining up behind a coordinated effort to stabilize flows through the Strait of Hormuz, including by sending additional…

articles.title: Bitcoin weakness deepens as war pushes traders to cut risk in BTC and stocks
articles.snippet: Bitcoin price remains rocky, and BTC and equities ETF outflows soar as the US and Israel-Iran war enters a fourth week.
articles.title_it: Bitcoin weakness deepens as war pushes traders to cut risk in BTC and stocks

articles.title: Bitcoin price aims to hold $70K amid rising inflation concerns
articles.snippet: Bitcoin searches for equilibrium at $70,000 while rising crude oil prices and tanking stock markets have investors worried over the future of inflation in the US.
articles.title_it: Bitcoin price aims to hold $70K amid rising inflation concerns
articles.title_en: Bitcoin price aims to hold $70K amid rising inflation concerns
